en.extract:
The yellow-bellied tit (Periparus venustulus) is a bird in the family Paridae. The species was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1870.
It is endemic to China. Its natural habitats are temperate forest and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest below 2000 meters of altitude. It is also present in urban green spaces.
Male yellow- bellied tits are very territorial, but they have a mutual respect for their neighbors. This is called the "dear- enemy" effect, since the birds respect each other's territory and want to maintain friendships.
Individuals tend to demonstrate a preference for one leg or the other ("footedness").


References


External links
Yellow-bellied tit at the Internet Bird Collection

The '''yellow-bellied tit''' ('''''Periparus venustulus''''') is a bird in the family [[Paridae]]. The species was [[Species description|first described]] by [[Robert Swinhoe]] in 1870.

It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[China]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[temperate forest]] and [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] below 2000 meters of altitude.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Li |first=Xiaoxi |last2=Ou |first2=Xiaoyang |last3=Sun |first3=Xingyue |last4=Li |first4=Haoran |last5=Li |first5=Yixiao |last6=Zheng |first6=Xi |date=August 2024 |title=Urban biodiversity conservation: A framework for ecological network construction and priority areas identification considering habit differences within species |journal=Journal of Environmental Management |volume=365 |article-number=121512 |doi=10.1016/j.jenvman.2024.121512 |issn=0301-4797|doi-access=free }}</ref> It is also present in urban green spaces.<ref name=":0" />

Male yellow- bellied tits are very territorial, but they have a mutual respect for their neighbors. This is called the "dear- enemy" effect, since the birds respect each other's territory and want to maintain friendships.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Wei|first=Min|last2=Lloyd|first2=Huw|last3=Zhang|first3=Yanyun|date=2011-04-01|title=Neighbour–stranger discrimination by Yellow-bellied Tit Parus venustulus: evidence for the "dear-enemy" effect|journal=Journal of Ornithology|language=en|volume=152|issue=2|pages=431–438|doi=10.1007/s10336-010-0609-6|issn=2193-7206}}</ref>

Individuals tend to demonstrate a preference for one leg or the other ("footedness").<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Yu |first=Gaoyang |last2=Guo |first2=Jinxin |last3=Xie |first3=Wenqian |last4=Wang |first4=Jun |last5=Wu |first5=Yichen |last6=Zhang |first6=Jinggang |last7=Xu |first7=Jiliang |last8=Li |first8=Jianqiang |date=2020-04-16 |title=Footedness predicts escape performance in a passerine bird |journal=Ecology and Evolution |volume=10 |issue=10 |pages=4251–4260 |doi=10.1002/ece3.6193 |issn=2045-7758|pmc=7246196 }}</ref>

zh.extract:
黄腹山雀（学名：Pardaliparus venustulus）为山雀科山雀属的鸟类，俗名采花鸟、黄豆崽、黄点儿，是中国的特有物种。分布于甘肃、陕西、河南、山西、湖北、湖南、江苏、上海、浙江、江西、安徽、福建、广东、广西、四川、贵州、河北、北京等地，一般栖息于海拔500-2000米的山地、冬季在平原也可见到、常活动于高大的针叶树和阔叶树上以及或灌丛间。该物种的模式产地在四川奉节至湖北宜昌。
